Recession Timing Dashboard

ActuallyFinance Home

This dashboard provides the current inputs to a timing strategy inspired by Philosophical Economics Growth-Trend Timing strategies. Backtested results for the strategy, and variations of the original proposed GTT strategies are also provided below. Economic indicators contribute signal scores based on the latest macro data; when the selected strategy's signal reaches its trigger, the portfolio switches from buy-and-hold to the selected moving-average trend rule.

Stats are computed from daily returns. Idle capital earns the cash proxy return; Sharpe and Sortino are calculated on excess returns over that same cash series. Trading costs are set to 0 bps.

Selected Strategy Rules

Score-based economic gate. Unemployment Rate Trend counts as 2; retail sales, industrial production, real income, and housing starts count as 1; timing turns on at score 2.

  1. Stay invested by default.
  2. Turn timing on when the selected indicators sum to a score of at least 2.
  3. When timing is on, hold the SPY ETF only when price is above its 200-day simple moving average, checked daily.
  4. When timing is off, ignore the moving-average rule and remain invested.

Indicators used by this strategy (links to FRED):

  • Unemployment Rate Trend; score 2.
  • Real Retail Sales Growth; score 1.
  • Industrial Production Growth; score 1.
  • Real Personal Income Growth; score 1.
  • Housing Starts Growth; score 1.

Backtest is structured to use FRED real-time revision events when available. Before an indicator's vintage history begins, final revised FRED values are used with an approximate one-month reporting lag; indicators do not contribute before their own series has enough history. The SPY ETF/Proxy uses a synthetic S&P 500 total-return approximation through 1987, the Yahoo ^SP500TR daily total-return index from 1988 until SPY starts, and adjusted SPY prices after inception. The pre-1988 synthetic segment is reduced by an inception-era SPY expense assumption. The historical test begins once the selected moving average is available. No explicit bid/ask trading cost is applied.
